message:
feat(db): mailbox health verdict + archive snapshot fallback + sidecar/checkpoint helper extraction

Substantial db-crate work that introduces a "live SQLite is suspect"
verdict and the supporting infrastructure to fall back to the
filesystem archive snapshot when the live database is corrupt or
significantly behind the archive. Touches every db source file
because the verdict and its consumers reach into forensics, mailbox
verdict, migration, queries, reconstruction, and the search planner.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs — extracted helpers

- New `archive_metadata_advantage_is_decisive(...)` decides whether
  archive-only metadata drift (more projects/agents in the archive,
  or missing-archive-projects on disk) should override live-db state.
  The check is conservative: it only returns true when the live db
  does NOT also have newer message evidence (more messages or a
  larger max message id). Otherwise the live db is treated as
  authoritative even when its project/agent counts lag.

- New `archive_storage_root_is_authoritative_for_sqlite_path(...)`
  decides whether the on-disk archive root should be treated as the
  authoritative read surface for a given SQLite path. Used by tools/
  and server/ to swap to archive snapshot reads transparently.

- New private path helpers:
    - `sqlite_path_with_suffix(path, suffix)`
    - `os_string_with_suffix(value, suffix)`
    - `sqlite_path_with_file_name_suffix(path, suffix, fallback)`
    - `os_str_starts_with(value, prefix)`
  All exist to replace the lossy
  `format!("{}{}", path.display(), suffix)` pattern with byte-exact
  `OsString` extension that preserves non-UTF8 file names. The
  `.bak` backup path computed by `DbPool::backup_db_to_bak()` now
  routes through `sqlite_path_with_file_name_suffix` so non-UTF8
  basenames are preserved.

- Reorganized health probes:
    - `sqlite_primary_read_path_is_healthy(path)` — fast probe
      that opens the primary db file and runs a sentinel query.
    - `sqlite_compatibility_read_path_is_healthy(path)` — slower
      compat probe that uses the read-only attach path.
    - `sqlite_file_is_healthy_with_compat_probe(path, primary_probe)`
      — generic helper that runs `primary_probe` first and only
      escalates to the compat probe when the primary probe trips a
      "file is encrypted or is not a database" / "malformed" error.
    - `normalize_compatibility_probe_result(...)` translates the
      compat probe's possible outcomes into a uniform Result so the
      caller does not have to special-case probe-not-applicable.

- Extracted `wal_checkpoint_truncate_path(db_path)` from the body of
  `DbPool::wal_checkpoint`. The original method was opening its own
  connection, setting `busy_timeout`, running
  `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`, and parsing the result row by
  hand. Lift that logic out so it can be called from
  `mcp_agent_mail_core::legacy::checkpoint_sqlite_for_copy` (which
  used to duplicate the same dance) and from any other future caller
  that needs to checkpoint a path-only db without going through the
  pool. `DbPool::wal_checkpoint` becomes a one-line forwarder.
  `wal_checkpoint_row_i64` and `parse_wal_checkpoint_rows` are
  shared between both checkpoint paths so the row parsing only
  exists in one place.

- `inspect_mailbox_db_inventory` now special-cases `:memory:`
  databases at the top so a doctor or test that points at an
  in-memory store does not blow up trying to stat the path.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/mailbox_verdict.rs

- `verdict_prefers_archive_snapshot_reads(verdict)` returns true
  when the verdict says the live db is suspect AND the archive has
  enough metadata to answer the request — this is the trigger that
  routes resource/tool reads to the archive snapshot.
- `verdict_prefers_archive_snapshot_reads_for_primary_read_surface(verdict)`
  is the stricter form used by the primary read path: it ALSO
  requires that the live db is missing the surface entirely (not
  just stale).
- `MailboxHealthVerdict` itself gains the new shape these helpers
  read against, plus signals for "live db has newer messages",
  "archive has more projects", "missing archive projects", etc.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/forensics.rs

- `is_in_memory_db_path(path)` — returns true for `:memory:` and the
  `file::memory:` URI form. Used to short-circuit forensic bundling
  for memory-only doctors.
- `forensic_bundle_dir_component(db_path)` — produces a stable,
  filesystem-safe directory component name for forensic bundles
  (database file basename when present, or `memory_db` for
  `:memory:` databases).
- `not_applicable_source_status(detail)` — uniform "this source is
  not applicable for forensic bundling" status for the JSON output.
  Used when an in-memory or missing source would otherwise leave the
  forensic JSON with a misleading "ok" status.
- Substantial expansion of the per-source bundling logic to honor
  the new in-memory short-circuit and to produce richer per-source
  status fields when the source is skipped vs. failed vs. succeeded.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/reconstruct.rs

- `malformed_attachments_json()` returns the canonical sentinel
  string the reconstruction path emits when the live archive's
  attachments JSON is unparseable. The same sentinel is recognized
  by tools/, server/mail_ui, and cli/robot when rendering, so the
  user sees a consistent "[malformed attachments]" placeholder
  instead of dumping raw bytes.
- `normalize_archive_attachments_json` and
  `normalize_archive_recipients_json` clean up the JSON shapes the
  archive emits before they enter the reconstruction pipeline,
  rejecting unrecognized variants and folding case-insensitive keys.
- `parse_salvaged_attachments_json` parses the archive's salvaged
  attachments output and returns a uniform list shape regardless of
  whether the underlying JSON was an array, object, or string.
- `normalize_reconstructed_required_agent_field`,
  `normalize_reconstructed_attachments_policy`,
  `normalize_reconstructed_contact_policy`,
  `reconstructed_recipient_field_is_valid`,
  `reconstructed_recipients_payload_is_valid` validate and
  normalize policy fields on reconstructed mailbox records, so a
  partially-corrupt archive cannot inject invalid policy values
  into the rebuilt db.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs

New batch of read-only helpers used by the verdict + reconstruct
paths to summarize archive-vs-live state without holding write
locks. Added without changing the existing query surface.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/migrate.rs

Migration runner now respects the verdict: when the live db is
flagged suspect, migrations skip destructive replays and instead
emit a structured "would migrate, deferred to reconstruction"
status so the doctor can route through the reconstruction path
rather than partially upgrading a corrupt db.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_planner.rs, search_recipes.rs, search_service.rs

The search planner and its recipe set learn to consult the verdict
before picking a search source. When the live db is suspect, the
planner switches to the archive snapshot for the affected projects
instead of returning a half-corrupt result set. `search_service`
gains the orchestration that wires the planner output to either
the live db or the archive snapshot reader.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/lib.rs

Re-exports the new pool helpers (`wal_checkpoint_truncate_path`,
`archive_metadata_advantage_is_decisive`,
`archive_storage_root_is_authoritative_for_sqlite_path`,
`sqlite_primary_read_path_is_healthy`,
`sqlite_compatibility_read_path_is_healthy`) plus the verdict
helpers so downstream crates can consume them via
`mcp_agent_mail_db::...` instead of reaching into module paths.
